intro:
I couldn't find any reliable information about the plant "Eremolithia rixfordii." However, I can suggest that you are possibly referring to the plant "Eremophila rixfordii" (also known as "Foothill Eremophila" or "Foothill Emu Bush").

Here is a paragraph about this plant:

Eremophila rixfordii is a shrubby, perennial plant native to the southwestern United States, belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, pain relief, and skin conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eremophila rixfordii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eremophila rixfordii is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, with its extracts also being used in modern pharmaceutical applications.
